name: The Elvenking, Thranduil
description:
Thranduil, Son of Oropher, was raised in Doriath and moved to Eryn Galen with his family after The War of Wrath. His father was named King by the Sylvan Elves due to their Sindarin heritage, therefore Thranduil himself became Prince of The Woodland Realm. King Oropher chose to keep moving his people further and further North, eventually settling in Emyn Duir. In doing so he kept himself and his people out of range of the Dwarves of Khazad-dûm, and avoid the threat of Mordor.

King Oropher understood that there would be no peace until The Servant of Morgoth was defeated. He therefore answered the summons for The Last Alliance of Elves and Men, but was unfortunately slain during The Battle of Dagorlad.

That was when Thranduil took his place as King of The Woodland Realm.

The following is the writers headcanon based on many factors:

In the days after Thranduil graduated from just another Sindar Ellon in Doriath to Prince of The Woodland Realm, he was headstrong and cocky and chose to lead many of their warriors North to investigate the threat of a dragon. King Oropher did not oppose, but he insisted on the mission being strictly recon.

Once The Prince and his men located the dragon's whereabouts, Thranduil was bent on slaying the beast. It appeared to be on route to The Greenwood, and was already too close for comfort, and it was his duty to protect his people. This is the Ellon who would one day be The Father of Legolas, and he was nearly his future son's equal when it came to skill in battle. He was not wrong to have such confidence in his own abilities.

Thranduil and his warriors had waited for the dragon to fall asleep, and silently made their way into it's cave. There was of course, no manner in which to kill a dragon this size with one blow, and the beast awoke to take on the onslaught of Elven Warriors. Alas, the headstrong Prince and his comrades underestimated the strength of the beasts scales, and the sheer force needed to kill a full fledged dragon. They did their best to prevent the beast dousing them with flame, but it was to no avail. Even a severely wounded dragon was able to muster up the strength for that. Thranduil was shielded by his warriors and his royal armor, interwoven with mithril. He stood in dismay and panic as his warriors fell all around him, but he refused to let them die in vain.

The Prince of The Woodland Realm stared into the eyes of this great beast, and just as it opened it's maw to close around him, Thranduil managed one last thrust of his longsword...strait through the soft palate of the dragon's mouth, piercing it's brain. The dying creature seemed determined to take his slayers down with him, and breathed one last breath of flame. The Sindarin Prince lifted one arm and managed to shield most of his face and his right eye, but a great deal of damage was done. He would wear these scars for the rest of his life.

With the help of Lord Elrond, and many centuries of treatment and rehabilitation (and all the trials and tribulations that come with, to say the least), Thranduil was once again on his feet, and wielding his sword. While The Peredhel was able to give The Prince of The Woodland Realm a glamour to cover his injuries and scarring, not to mention assist with any residual pain, he could not restore Thranduil's vision. His unshielded left eye was completely white with blindness, and the right did not come out of the ordeal unscathed. He can see shadows and light, and some semblance of his surroundings, but was forced to learn to hone his other senses in order to compensate.

Any Elf has far more heightened senses than the mor
creator: Loki_The_Trickster_God
